subject: [PATCH 1/3] iova: convert from rbtree to maple tree

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch replaces the hand-rolled red-black tree in the IOVA allocator with a Maple tree, a B-tree variant designed for range tracking and gap searching. The change reduces memory usage by shrinking the size of struct iova from 40 bytes to 16 bytes and improves search performance by using O(log_10 n) search depth and better cache locality. The patch also simplifies the code by eliminating the need for retry loops and successor gap fixups, and introduces a new allocation strategy that over-requests memory to guarantee alignment. Overall, this patch aims to improve both memory efficiency and search performance in the IOVA allocator.
